title: BSET Tutorial
text: BSET (Bayesian Surrogate Evaluation Test) R package assessing validity surrogate markers clinical trials. provides hypothesis testing tools evaluate whether surrogate can reliably estimate causal effect treatment primary outcome. package implements imputation-based Bayesian methodology Carlotti Parast (2026), extending frequentist rank-based approach Parast et al. (2024). BSET addresses key limitations frequentist method, including lack causal interpretability inability adjust covariates estimation process. package supports Bayesian testing without baseline covariates. Additionally, includes comprehensive simulation suites replicate studies papers, enabling performance comparisons Bayesian frequentist approaches across diverse clinical scenarios.

title: BSET Tutorial
text: package yet available CRAN, can install development version BSET GitHub : follow along examples tutorial, also need install following packages:
code: # install.packages("pak") # pak::pak("PietroCarlotti/BSET") library(ggplot2) library(dplyr)

title: BSET Tutorial
text: section provides minimal working example readers want apply BSET data right away. two main functions BSET_no_X, runs test without adjusting covariates, BSET_X, adjusts baseline covariate. require data frame columns primary outcome Y, surrogate S, treatment assignment Z. BSET_X additionally requires covariate column X. illustrate usage package, simulate small dataset binary covariate: run BSET without adjusting covariates, pass data frame names relevant columns: Posterior distribution θ\theta BSET_no_X. run BSET baseline covariate, add covariate column name via X argument: Posterior distribution θ\theta BSET_X, adjusted baseline covariate. functions return posterior distribution θ\theta, discrepancy treatment effects YY SS. blue vertical line marks upper bound 95% credible interval, green vertical line marks validation threshold η\eta, orange vertical line marks true value δ\delta (frequentist estimand Parast et al. (2024)), red vertical line marks true value θ\theta. blue line falls green line, evidence surrogate valid. rest tutorial explains data generated, estimands δ\delta θ\theta computed, validation threshold η\eta calibrated.
code: # Set the random seed for reproducibility set.seed(1234)  # Sample size n <- 50  # Binary covariate X <- rbinom(n, 1, 0.5)  # Treatment assignment Z <- rbinom(n, 1, 0.5)  # Primary outcome Y <- 2 * Z -3 * X + rnorm(n)  # Surrogate outcome S <- Y + rnorm(n)  df <- data.frame(   Y = Y,   S = S,   Z = Z,   X = X ) result_no_X <- BSET::BSET_no_X(   data = df,   Y = "Y",   S = "S",   Z = "Z",   seed = 123,   plot = TRUE ) result_no_X$theta_posterior_plot result_X <- BSET::BSET_X(   data = df,   Y = "Y",   S = "S",   Z = "Z",   X = "X",   seed = 123,   plot = TRUE ) result_X$theta_posterior_plot

title: BSET Tutorial
text: start setting random seed reproducibility results. package includes two functions generate data simulations: DGP DGP_X. first one generates data simulation settings Parast et al. (2024), include covariates; second one generates data depends binary covariate XX used simulations Carlotti Parast (2026). example, can generate data second setting defined Parast et al. (2024) (.e.: setting surrogate perfect covariates included): can generate data first setting defined Carlotti Parast (2026) (.e.: setting surrogate useful covariates included):
code: # Set the random seed for reproducibility set.seed(1234)  # Sample size and treatment assignment probability used throughout n <- 50 p <- 0.5 # Mean vector of potential outcomes for the primary outcome and the surrogate mu_star <- c(6, 6, 2.5, 2.5)  # Covariance matrix of potential outcomes for the primary outcome and the surrogate Sigma_star <- kronecker(   diag(2),   matrix(     data = c(3, 3,              3, 3.1),     nrow = 2,     ncol = 2) )  # Generate the data data_no_X <- BSET::DGP_no_X(   n = n,   p = p,   mu_star = mu_star,   Sigma_star = Sigma_star,   model = "Gaussian" ) # Binary covariate probability q <- 0.5  # Mean vector for potential outcomes when X = 0 and when X = 1 mu_0 <- c(5, 5, 0, 0) mu_1 <- c(5, -5, 0, -10)  # Covariance matrix for potential outcomes when X = 0 and when X = 1 Sigma_0 <- kronecker(   diag(2),   matrix(     data = c(1, 1,              1, 2),     nrow = 2,     ncol = 2)   )  Sigma_1 <- kronecker(   diag(2),   matrix(     data = c(1, 1,              1, 2),     nrow = 2,     ncol = 2)   )  # Generate the data data_X <- BSET::DGP_X_binary(   n = n,   p = p,   q = q,   mu_0 = mu_0,   mu_1 = mu_1,   Sigma_0 = Sigma_0,   Sigma_1 = Sigma_1   )

what: Computing the true values of the discrepancy parameters
title: BSET Tutorial
text: Given data generated functions, can estimate δ\delta θ\theta using compute_delta compute_theta functions, respectively. example, data generated without covariates: data generated covariates: estimated values δ\delta θ\theta shown table . Estimated values δ\delta θ\theta two settings. determine values δ\delta θ\theta settings defined two papers, use large-scale Monte Carlo simulation via compute_estimands_Parast_et_al_2024 function data without covariates (Parast et al. 2024), compute_estimands_Carlotti_and_Parast_2026 function data including covariates (Carlotti Parast 2026). functions run Monte Carlo simulation large number samples (e.g., 1,000,000) can called follows: purpose tutorial, load precomputed versions shipped package: shown table , δ\delta θ\theta yield identical values (Monte Carlo error) across settings. estimands close 00 perfect surrogate, significantly higher useless surrogate, take intermediate values imperfect surrogates misspecified models. Monte Carlo estimates δ\delta θ\theta simulation settings considered Parast et al. (2024). Whereas, shown table , include covariates data generating process, δ\delta θ\theta yield different values. particular, θ\theta close 00 perfect surrogate, δ\delta significantly higher. Monte Carlo estimates δ\delta θ\theta simulation settings considered Carlotti Parast (2026).
code: # Compute delta delta_no_X <- BSET::compute_delta(MC_data = data_no_X)  # Compute theta theta_no_X <- BSET::compute_theta(MC_data = data_no_X) # Compute delta delta_X <- BSET::compute_delta(MC_data = data_X)  # Compute theta theta_X <- BSET::compute_theta(MC_data = data_X) # Compute Monte Carlo estimands (based on 1,000,000 samples) — this is slow estimands_Parast_et_al_2024 <- BSET::compute_estimands_Parast_et_al_2024(n_MC = 1000000) estimands_Carlotti_and_Parast_2026 <- BSET::compute_estimands_Carlotti_and_Parast_2026(n_MC = 1000000) # Load precomputed Monte Carlo estimands (based on 1,000,000 samples) estimands_Parast_et_al_2024 <- BSET::estimands_Parast_et_al_2024 estimands_Carlotti_and_Parast_2026 <- BSET::estimands_Carlotti_and_Parast_2026

title: BSET Tutorial
text: validation threshold η\eta value θ\theta surrogate considered valid. explained Carlotti Parast (2026), computation η\eta based distribution following Bayes factor: BFn=1−FBeta(+nV̂S,b+n−nV̂S)(12)1−FBeta(,b)(12).   BF_{n} = \frac{1 - F_{\text{Beta} \left( + n \hat{V}_{S}, \, b + n - n \hat{V}_{S} \right)} \left( \frac{1}{2} \right)}{1 - F_{\text{Beta} \left( , \, b \right)} \left( \frac{1}{2} \right)}.  Given true value VSV_S, distribution BFnBF_n can computed function compute_BF_distribution. example, distribution Bayes factor null hypothesis VS=0.5V_S = 0.5 can computed follows: distribution Bayes factor null hypothesis shown figure . Since Bayes factor can take values wide range, plot distribution log scale. Distribution Bayes factor null hypothesis VS=0.5V_S = 0.5. Given distribution Bayes factor null hypothesis, can compute critical value BFn,αBF_{n, \alpha} corresponding specified Type error rate α\alpha. example, α=0.05\alpha = 0.05 BFn,α=1.385.   BF_{n, \alpha} = 1.385. value BFn,αBF_{n, \alpha}, can compute value vSv_S satisfies following equation: P(BFn≥BFn,α|VS=vS)=1−β,   P(\text{BF}_n \geq \text{BF}_{n, \alpha} \; | \; V_S = v_S) = 1 - \beta, 1−β1 - \beta desired power test. root-finding algorithm used solve value vSv_S satisfies equation, implemented function compute_V_S_star. example, Type II error rate β=0.2\beta = 0.2, can compute value vSv_S follows: , vS=0.685.   v_S = 0.685. Finally, validation threshold η\eta can computed η=max⁡{vY−vS,0},   \eta = \max \{v_Y - v_S, 0\}, vYv_Y hypothesized value treatment effect primary outcome (typically set equal estimate computed available data). example, can set vYv_Y equal Monte Carlo estimate VYV_Y setting 1 Parast et al. (2024) (.e.: setting surrogate perfect covariates included). case, η=0.238.   \eta = 0.238.
code: # Hypothesized value of V_S under the null V_S_zero <- 0.5  # Prior parameters for the Beta distribution a <- 1 b <- 1  # Alternative hypothesis for the Bayes factor BF_alternative <- "greater"  # Compute the distribution of the Bayes factor BF_distribution <- BSET::compute_BF_distribution(   n = n,   V_S_true = V_S_zero,   V_S_zero = V_S_zero,   a = a,   b = b,   BF_alternative = BF_alternative ) # Type I error rate alpha <- 0.05  # Compute the critical value of the Bayes factor corresponding to alpha BF_alpha <- BF_distribution %>%     dplyr::filter(BF_CDF >= 1 - alpha) %>%     dplyr::slice(1) %>%     dplyr::pull(BF_values) # Type II error rate beta <- 0.2  # Compute the value of v_S that satisfies the power constraint V_S_star <- BSET::compute_V_S_star(   n = n,   alpha = alpha,   beta = beta,   V_S_zero = V_S_zero,   a = a,   b = b,   BF_alternative = BF_alternative,   root_tolerance = 1e-16 )$V_S_star # Hypothesized value of the treatment effect on the primary outcome v_Y <- estimands_Parast_et_al_2024$V_Y_MC[2]  # Compute the validation threshold eta eta <- max(v_Y - V_S_star, 0)

title: BSET Tutorial
text: package includes two functions run BSET procedure: BSET_no_X BSET_X. first one runs BSET procedure without adjusting covariates, second one runs BSET procedure adjusting covariates. result procedure summarized posterior distribution θ\theta 95% credible interval. general, upper bound credible interval falls validation threshold η\eta, BSET procedure concludes evidence surrogate valid. instead upper bound exceeds η\eta, procedure find sufficient evidence surrogacy.

title: BSET Tutorial
text: example, can run BSET procedure without adjusting covariates data generated second setting Parast et al. (2024). First , need prepare data format required function BSET_no_X: data frame three columns, first column contains observed values primary outcome YY, second column contains observed values surrogate SS, third column contains treatment assignment ZZ. also need define posterior sampling parameters: n_chains: number MCMC chains run. n_iter: total number MCMC iterations run chain. burn_in_ratio: proportion MCMC iterations discard burn-. , need define prior parameters distribution potential outcomes Bayesian model. particular, need specify: mu_0: numeric vector length 4 containing prior means potential outcomes. Sigma_0: numeric positive-definite matrix dimension 4×44 \times 4 containing prior covariance matrix potential outcomes. s: numeric vector length 4 containing prior scale parameters potential outcomes. tau: numeric scalar containing prior scale parameter correlation matrix potential outcomes. true values δ\delta θ\theta known, can also specify input function BSET_no_X check whether included posterior credible intervals. case, can set δ\delta θ\theta equal Monte Carlo estimates setting 2 Parast et al. (2024). can also specify additional parameters BSET procedure, : seed: integer scalar set random seed reproducibility MCMC sampling process. plot: logical scalar indicating whether plot posterior distribution θ\theta. mute: logical scalar indicating whether suppress output MCMC sampling process. parallel: logical scalar indicating whether run MCMC sampling process parallel sequentially. Finally, can run BSET procedure without adjusting covariates follows: posterior distribution θ\theta BSET procedure without adjusting covariates shown figure . Posterior distribution θ\theta BSET procedure without adjusting covariates. blue vertical line indicates upper bound 95% credible interval, green vertical line indicates value validation threshold η\eta, orange vertical line indicates true value δ\delta, red vertical line indicates true value θ\theta. Two features result worth highlighting. First, posterior distribution θ\theta centered around true value θ\theta, demonstrating Bayesian imputation approach accurately recovers true discrepancy treatment effects YY SS. setting, covariates play role data generating process, frequentist estimand δ\delta coincides θ\theta methods perform similarly. Second, since upper bound 95% credible interval falls validation threshold η\eta, BSET procedure concludes evidence surrogate valid setting.
code: # Prepare the data for BSET_no_X BSET_no_X_data <- data.frame(   Y = data_no_X$P_observed[, "Y"],   S = data_no_X$P_observed[, "S"],   Z = data_no_X$Z  ) # Posterior sampling parameters n_chains <- 2 n_iter <- 2000 burn_in_ratio <- 0.25 # Prior parameters mu_0 <- rep(0, 4) Sigma_0 <- diag(4) s <- rep(1, 4) tau <- 1 # Run the BSET procedure without adjusting for covariates BSET_no_X_results <- BSET::BSET_no_X(   data = BSET_no_X_data,   Y = "Y",   S = "S",   Z = "Z",   delta_true = estimands_Parast_et_al_2024$delta_MC[2],   theta_true = estimands_Parast_et_al_2024$theta_MC[2],   seed = 123,   n_chains = n_chains,   n_iter = n_iter,   burn_in_ratio = burn_in_ratio,   a = a,   b = b,   alpha = alpha,   beta = beta,   V_S_zero = V_S_zero,   BF_alternative = BF_alternative,   root_tolerance = 1e-16,   mu_0 = mu_0,   Sigma_0 = Sigma_0,   s = s,   tau = tau,   plot = TRUE,   mute = TRUE,   parallel = TRUE )

title: BSET Tutorial
text: example, can run BSET procedure adjusting covariates data generated first setting Carlotti Parast (2026). First , need prepare data format required function BSET_X: data frame four columns, first column contains observed values primary outcome YY, second column contains observed values surrogate SS, third column contains treatment assignment ZZ, fourth column contains values binary covariate XX. call dd number covariates, case equal 22 (including intercept). posterior sampling parameters defined way BSET procedure without adjusting covariates. Whereas prior parameters distribution potential outcomes Bayesian model defined follows: mu_beta: numeric vector length dd containing prior means regression coefficients potential outcomes covariates. Sigma_beta: numeric positive-definite matrix dimension d×dd \times d containing prior covariance matrix regression coefficients potential outcomes covariates. can also specify true values δ\delta θ\theta input function BSET_X check included posterior credible intervals. case, can set δ\delta θ\theta equal Monte Carlo estimates δ\delta θ\theta setting 1 Carlotti Parast (2026). Finally, can run BSET procedure adjusting covariates follows: posterior distribution θ\theta BSET procedure adjusting covariates shown figure . Posterior distribution θ\theta BSET procedure adjusting covariates. blue vertical line indicates upper bound 95% credible interval, green vertical line indicates value validation threshold η\eta, orange vertical line indicates true value δ\delta, red vertical line indicates true value θ\theta. Unlike previous setting, covariates now play role data generating process, two methods diverge. posterior distribution θ\theta still centered around true value, confirming covariate-adjusted Bayesian model correctly recovers true discrepancy parameter. contrast, frequentist estimand δ\delta diverges substantially true θ\theta, illustrating key limitation rank-based methods covariates present. Since upper bound 95% credible interval falls validation threshold η\eta, BSET procedure correctly concludes evidence surrogate valid setting, frequentist method, based δ\delta, incorrectly conclude .
code: # Prepare the data for BSET_X BSET_X_data <- data.frame(   Y = data_X$P_observed[, "Y"],   S = data_X$P_observed[, "S"],   Z = data_X$Z,   X = data_X$X ) # Prior parameters for the regression coefficients of the potential outcomes on the covariates d <- 2 mu_beta <- rep(0, d) Sigma_beta <- 10*diag(d) # Run the BSET procedure adjusting for covariates BSET_X_results <- BSET::BSET_X(   data = BSET_X_data,   Y = "Y",   S = "S",   Z = "Z",   X = "X",   delta_true = estimands_Carlotti_and_Parast_2026$delta_MC[1],   theta_true = estimands_Carlotti_and_Parast_2026$theta_MC[1],   seed = 123,   n_chains = n_chains,   n_iter = n_iter,   burn_in_ratio = burn_in_ratio,   a = a,   b = b,   alpha = alpha,   beta = beta,   V_S_zero = V_S_zero,   BF_alternative = BF_alternative,   root_tolerance = 1e-16,   mu_beta = mu_beta,   Sigma_beta = Sigma_beta,   s = s,   tau = tau,   plot = TRUE,   mute = TRUE,   parallel = TRUE )

title: Authors and Citation
text: Pietro Carlotti. Maintainer.

code: @Manual{,   title = {BSET: A Bayesian Surrogate Evaluation Test},   author = {Pietro Carlotti},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 1.0, https://github.com/PietroCarlotti/BSET},   url = {https://pietrocarlotti.github.io/BSET/}, }

title: A Bayesian Surrogate Evaluation Test
text: BSET (Bayesian Surrogate Evaluation Test) R package assessing validity surrogate markers clinical trials. provides hypothesis testing tools evaluate whether surrogate can reliably estimate causal effect treatment primary outcome. package implements imputation-based Bayesian methodology Carlotti Parast (2026), extending frequentist rank-based approach Parast et al. (2024). BSET addresses key limitations frequentist method, including lack causal interpretability inability adjust covariates estimation process. package supports Bayesian testing without baseline covariates. Additionally, includes comprehensive simulation suites replicate studies papers, enabling performance comparisons Bayesian frequentist approaches across diverse clinical scenarios. detailed walkthrough examples imputation-based methodology, please visit BSET Tutorial.

what: Bayesian Surrogate Evaluation Test with Covariates from Carlotti and Parast (2026) — BSET_X
title: Bayesian Surrogate Evaluation Test with Covariates from Carlotti and Parast (2026) — BSET_X
text: function implements Bayesian Surrogate Evaluation Test (BSET) includes covariates via multivariate regression model potential, proposed Carlotti Parast (2026) . function fits Bayesian model using Stan generate posterior samples parameters interest, including regression coefficients covariates. posterior samples used conduct Bayesian hypothesis test evaluating validity surrogate marker, adjusting covariates. frequentist test also performed comparison. Bayesian model specified follows: $$(Y_i, S_i) = \begin{cases} P_{1i}, & \text{} Z_i = 1 \\ P_{0i}, & \text{} Z_i = 0 \end{cases}, \quad = 1, \ldots, n,$$ $$P_i \overset{\text{ind.}}{\sim} \mathcal{N}_4(B X_i, \Sigma), \quad = 1, \ldots, n,$$ $$B = \begin{bmatrix} \beta_{1} & \beta_{2} & \beta_{3} & \beta_{4} \\ \end{bmatrix}^\top,$$ $$\beta_{k} \sim \mathcal{N}_d (\mu_{\beta}, \Sigma_{\beta}), \quad k = 1, \ldots, 4,$$ $$\Sigma = \text{diag}(\sigma_{1:4}) \, \Omega \, \text{diag}(\sigma_{1:4}),$$ $$\sigma_k \sim \text{Half-Normal}(0, s_k), \quad k = 1, \ldots, 4,$$ $$\Omega \sim \text{LKJ}(\tau).$$ primary user-facing function package includes working example .

title: Bayesian Surrogate Evaluation Test with Covariates from Carlotti and Parast (2026) — BSET_X
code: BSET_X(   data,   Y,   S,   Z,   X,   delta_true = NULL,   theta_true = NULL,   seed = NULL,   n_chains = 4,   n_iter = 2000,   burn_in_ratio = 0.25,   a = 1,   b = 1,   alpha = 0.05,   beta = 0.2,   V_S_zero = 0.5,   BF_alternative = "greater",   root_tolerance = 1e-16,   intercept = TRUE,   mu_beta = NULL,   Sigma_beta = NULL,   s = rep(1, 4),   tau = 1,   plot = FALSE,   mute = TRUE,   parallel = TRUE )

title: Bayesian Surrogate Evaluation Test with Covariates from Carlotti and Parast (2026) — BSET_X
text: data data frame containing observed data. Y Character. Name outcome variable. S Character. Name surrogate variable. Z Character. Name treatment assignment variable. X Character vector. Names covariates include model. delta_true true value delta, used calculate frequentist coverage simulations (optional). theta_true true value theta, used calculate Bayesian coverage simulations (optional). seed Random seed reproducibility (optional. provided, random seed generated). n_chains Number MCMC chains run (default 4). n_iter Number iterations per MCMC chain (default 2000). burn_in_ratio Proportion iterations discard burn-(default 0.25). , b Prior parameters prior Beta distribution V_S (default = 1, b = 1). alpha Type error rate test (default 0.05). beta Type II error rate test (default 0.2). V_S_zero Value V_S null hypothesis (default 0.5). BF_alternative Alternative hypothesis Bayes factor test ("greater", "less" "two.sided"). root_tolerance Numerical tolerance root-finding algorithms (default 1e-16). intercept Whether include intercept regression model (default TRUE). mu_beta Prior mean vector regression coefficients (default vector zeros length equal number covariates, including intercept specified). Sigma_beta Prior covariance matrix regression coefficients (default diagonal matrix large variances, dimensions equal number covariates, including intercept specified). s Prior scale parameters error variance (default vector ones length 4). tau Prior parameter LKJ correlation distribution (default 1). plot Logical. Whether plot posterior distribution theta (default FALSE). mute Logical. Whether suppress Stan output model fitting (default TRUE). parallel Logical. Whether use parallel processing MCMC sampling (default TRUE).

title: Bayesian Surrogate Evaluation Test with Covariates from Carlotti and Parast (2026) — BSET_X
text: Carlotti P, Parast L (2026). “Bayesian Critique Rank-Based Methods Surrogate Marker Evaluation.” arXiv preprint arXiv:2603.14381. Parast L, Cai T, Tian L (2024). “rank-based approach evaluate surrogate marker small sample setting.” Biometrics, 80(1), ujad035.

title: Bayesian Surrogate Evaluation Test with Covariates from Carlotti and Parast (2026) — BSET_X
code: # Generate data from the setting of Carlotti and Parast (2026) with a binary covariate set.seed(123) data_X <- DGP_X_binary(   n = 100,   p = 0.5,   q = 0.5,   mu_0 = c(5, 5, 0, 0),   mu_1 = c(5, -5, 0, -10),   Sigma_0 = kronecker(diag(2), matrix(c(1, 1, 1, 2), 2, 2)),   Sigma_1 = kronecker(diag(2), matrix(c(1, 1, 1, 2), 2, 2)) )  # Prepare the data frame df <- data.frame(   Y = data_X$P_observed[, "Y"],   S = data_X$P_observed[, "S"],   Z = data_X$Z,   X = data_X$X )  # Run BSET with covariates (computationally intensive) # \donttest{ result <- BSET_X(   data = df,   Y = "Y",   S = "S",   Z = "Z",   X = "X",   seed = 123,   n_chains = 2,   n_iter = 500 ) # }

what: Bayesian Surrogate Evaluation Test without Covariates from Carlotti and Parast (2026) — BSET_no_X
title: Bayesian Surrogate Evaluation Test without Covariates from Carlotti and Parast (2026) — BSET_no_X
text: function implements Bayesian Surrogate Evaluation Test (BSET) without covariates, proposed Carlotti Parast (2026) . function fits Bayesian model using Stan generate posterior samples parameters interest. posterior samples used conduct Bayesian hypothesis test evaluating validity surrogate marker. frequentist test also performed comparison. Bayesian model specified follows: $$(Y_i, S_i) = \begin{cases} P_{1i}, & \text{} Z_i = 1 \\ P_{0i}, & \text{} Z_i = 0 \end{cases}, \quad = 1, \ldots, n,$$ $$P_i \overset{\text{ind.}}{\sim} \mathcal{N}_4(\mu, \Sigma), \quad = 1, \ldots, n,$$ $$\mu \sim \mathcal{N}_4(\mu_0, \Sigma_0),$$ $$\Sigma = \text{diag}(\sigma_{1:4}) \, \Omega \, \text{diag}(\sigma_{1:4}),$$ $$\sigma_k \sim \text{Half-Normal}(0, s_k), \quad k = 1, \ldots, 4,$$ $$\Omega \sim \text{LKJ}(\tau).$$ primary user-facing function package includes working example .

title: Bayesian Surrogate Evaluation Test without Covariates from Carlotti and Parast (2026) — BSET_no_X
code: BSET_no_X(   data,   Y,   S,   Z,   delta_true = NULL,   theta_true = NULL,   seed = NULL,   n_chains = 4,   n_iter = 2000,   burn_in_ratio = 0.25,   a = 1,   b = 1,   alpha = 0.05,   beta = 0.2,   V_S_zero = 0.5,   BF_alternative = "greater",   root_tolerance = 1e-16,   mu_0 = rep(0, 4),   Sigma_0 = diag(4),   s = rep(1, 4),   tau = 1,   plot = FALSE,   mute = TRUE,   parallel = TRUE )

title: Bayesian Surrogate Evaluation Test without Covariates from Carlotti and Parast (2026) — BSET_no_X
text: data data frame containing observed data. Y Character. Name outcome variable. S Character. Name surrogate variable. Z Character. Name treatment assignment variable. delta_true true value delta, used calculate frequentist coverage simulations (optional). theta_true true value theta, used calculate Bayesian coverage simulations (optional). seed Random seed reproducibility (optional. provided, random seed generated). n_chains Number MCMC chains run (default 4). n_iter Number iterations per MCMC chain (default 2000). burn_in_ratio Proportion iterations discard burn-(default 0.25). , b Prior parameters prior Beta distribution V_S (default = 1, b = 1). alpha Type error rate test (default 0.05). beta Type II error rate test (default 0.2). V_S_zero Value V_S null hypothesis (default 0.5). BF_alternative Alternative hypothesis Bayes factor test ("greater", "less" "two.sided"). root_tolerance Numerical tolerance root-finding algorithms (default 1e-16). mu_0 Prior mean vector mean parameters (default vector zeros length 4). Sigma_0 Prior covariance matrix mean vector (default 4x4 identity matrix). s Prior scale parameters error variance (default vector ones length 4). tau Prior parameter LKJ correlation distribution (default 1). plot Logical. Whether plot posterior distribution theta (default FALSE). mute Logical. Whether suppress Stan output model fitting (default TRUE). parallel Logical. Whether use parallel processing MCMC sampling (default TRUE).

title: Bayesian Surrogate Evaluation Test without Covariates from Carlotti and Parast (2026) — BSET_no_X
text: list containing: stan_cores: number CPU cores used MCMC sampling. Bayesian_test: list containing results Bayesian test, including posterior samples credible intervals. frequentist_test: list containing results frequentist test, including point estimates confidence intervals. theta_posterior_plot: ggplot object showing posterior distribution \(\theta\), vertical lines indicating credible interval, \(\eta\) threshold, true values \(\delta\) \(\theta\) (provided).

title: Bayesian Surrogate Evaluation Test without Covariates from Carlotti and Parast (2026) — BSET_no_X
text: Carlotti P, Parast L (2026). “Bayesian Critique Rank-Based Methods Surrogate Marker Evaluation.” arXiv preprint arXiv:2603.14381. Parast L, Cai T, Tian L (2024). “rank-based approach evaluate surrogate marker small sample setting.” Biometrics, 80(1), ujad035.

title: Bayesian Surrogate Evaluation Test without Covariates from Carlotti and Parast (2026) — BSET_no_X
code: # Generate data from the perfect surrogate setting of Parast et al. (2024) set.seed(123) data_no_X <- DGP_no_X(   n = 100,   p = 0.5,   mu_star = c(6, 6, 2.5, 2.5),   Sigma_star = kronecker(diag(2), matrix(c(3, 3, 3, 3.1), 2, 2)),   model = "Gaussian" )  # Prepare the data frame df <- data.frame(   Y = data_no_X$P_observed[, "Y"],   S = data_no_X$P_observed[, "S"],   Z = data_no_X$Z )  # Run BSET without covariates (computationally intensive) # \donttest{ result <- BSET_no_X(   data = df,   Y = "Y",   S = "S",   Z = "Z",   seed = 123,   n_chains = 2,   n_iter = 500 ) # }

what: Bayesian Test from Carlotti and Parast (2026) — Bayesian_test
title: Bayesian Test from Carlotti and Parast (2026) — Bayesian_test
text: function performs Bayesian test surrogate evaluation based imputation-based methodology Carlotti Parast (2026) . calculates credible intervals, \(\eta\) threshold used, determines surrogate valid. function generally intended called directly user instead used internally within BSET_no_X BSET_X.

title: Bayesian Test from Carlotti and Parast (2026) — Bayesian_test
code: Bayesian_test(   P_MCMC,   alpha = 0.05,   V_S_star,   theta_true = NULL,   V_Y_true = NULL )

title: Bayesian Test from Carlotti and Parast (2026) — Bayesian_test
text: P_MCMC three-dimensional array potential outcomes obtained via MCMC sampling dimensions [n_subjects, variables, n_samples]. variables correspond \((Y_1, S_1, Y_0, S_0)\). alpha Numeric. Significance level credible interval (default 0.05). V_S_star Numeric. value \(v_S\) satisfies power constraint surrogate validation test. theta_true Numeric (optional). true value \(\eta\), used calculate frequentist coverage simulations. V_Y_true Numeric (optional). true value \(V_Y\), used calculate surrogate validation threshold \(\eta\) simulations. provided, estimated MCMC samples.

title: Bayesian Test from Carlotti and Parast (2026) — Bayesian_test
text: list containing: V_Y_MCMC: Posterior draws \(V_Y\). V_S_MCMC: Posterior draws \(V_S\). theta_MCMC: Posterior draws \(\theta = V_Y - V_S\). CI: calculated credible interval \(\theta\). eta: \(\eta\) threshold value used test. coverage: Logical indicating theta_true falls within CI (theta_true provided). power: Logical indicating upper bound CI eta, indicates test identifies surrogate valid.

title: Data Generating Process with a Gaussian Covariate — DGP_X_Gaussian
text: potential outcomes generated multivariate normal distribution mean vector covariance matrix depend value \(X\). Specifically, mean vector linear function \(X\): $$\mu(X) = x \cdot (\beta_{Y1}, \beta_{S1}, \beta_{Y0}, \beta_{S0})^T,$$ covariance matrix constant across values \(X\): $$\Sigma(X) = \Sigma.$$

what: Compute the Distribution of the Bayes Factor from Carlotti and Parast (2026) — compute_BF_distribution
title: Compute the Distribution of the Bayes Factor from Carlotti and Parast (2026) — compute_BF_distribution
text: function calculates probability mass function cumulative distribution function Bayes factor defined Carlotti Parast (2026)  following hypothesis test: $$\begin{cases} H_0: V_S = V_S^{0} \\ H_1: V_S > V_S^{0} \end{cases}$$ \(V_S\) surrogate's treatment effect \(S\) measured probability $$V_S = P(S_{1i} > S_{0i})$$ \(V_S^{0}\) hypothesized value null hypothesis. hypotheses can tested fitting following Beta-binomial model data: $$\hat{V}_S \mid V_S \sim \text{Binomial} (n, V_S)$$ $$V_S \sim \text{Beta} (, b),$$ \(\hat{V}_S\) sample estimate surrogate's treatment effect \(S\) computed $$\hat{V}_S = \frac{1}{n} \sum\limits^{n}_{=1} (S_{1i} > S_{0i}).$$ Bayes factor computed ratio marginal likelihoods alternatives: $$BF_{n} = \frac{1 - F_{\text{Beta} (+ n \hat{V}_S, b + n - n \hat{V}_S)} (\frac{1}{2})}{1 - F_{\text{Beta} (, b)} (\frac{1}{2})},$$ \(F_{\text{Beta}}\) cumulative distribution function Beta distribution, \(\) \(b\) shape parameters Beta prior. Given true value \(V_S\), distribution Bayes factor can computed evaluating \(BF_n\) possible values \(\hat{V}_S\) corresponding probabilities Binomial distribution parameters \(n\) true value \(V_S\). function generally intended called directly user instead used internally within BSET_no_X BSET_X.

title: Compute the Distribution of the Bayes Factor from Carlotti and Parast (2026) — compute_BF_distribution
code: compute_BF_distribution(   n,   V_S_true,   V_S_zero = 0.5,   a = 1,   b = 1,   BF_alternative )

title: Compute the Distribution of the Bayes Factor from Carlotti and Parast (2026) — compute_BF_distribution
text: n Integer. sample size. V_S_true Numeric. true value treatment effect surrogate. V_S_zero Numeric. hypothesized value surrogate's treatment effect null hypothesis (default 0.5). Numeric. First shape parameter alpha Beta prior (default 1). b Numeric. Second shape parameter beta Beta prior (default 1). BF_alternative Character. type alternative hypothesis: either "two_sided" "greater".

title: Compute the Distribution of the Bayes Factor from Carlotti and Parast (2026) — compute_BF_distribution
text: data frame containing: BF_values: possible values Bayes Factor. BF_PMF: probability mass function Bayes Factor. BF_CDF: cumulative distribution function Bayes Factor.

title: Compute the Distribution of the Bayes Factor from Carlotti and Parast (2026) — compute_BF_distribution
text: Carlotti P, Parast L (2026). “Bayesian Critique Rank-Based Methods Surrogate Marker Evaluation.” arXiv preprint arXiv:2603.14381.

what: Compute \(v_S\) from Carlotti and Parast (2026) — compute_V_S_star
title: Compute \(v_S\) from Carlotti and Parast (2026) — compute_V_S_star
text: function determines value \(v_S\) used compute surrogate validation threshold \(\eta\) Carlotti Parast (2026) : $$\eta = \max \{v_Y - v_S, 0\},$$ \(v_Y\) hypothesized value treatment effect primary outcome (typically set equal estimate computed available data) \(v_S\) value satisfies following power constraint: $$P(\text{BF}_n \geq \text{BF}_{n, \alpha} \; | \; V_S = v_S) = 1 - \beta,$$ \(\text{BF}_{n, \alpha}\) \((1 - \alpha)\) quantile Bayes factor distribution null hypothesis \(V_S = V^0_{S}\), \(1 - \beta\) desired power test. function computes distribution Bayes factor null hypothesis, derives critical value \(\text{BF}_{n, \alpha}\), uses root-finding algorithm solve value \(v_S\) satisfies power constraint. function generally intended called directly user instead used internally within BSET_no_X BSET_X.

title: Compute \(v_S\) from Carlotti and Parast (2026) — compute_V_S_star
code: compute_V_S_star(   n,   alpha = 0.05,   beta = 0.2,   V_S_zero = 0.5,   a = 1,   b = 1,   BF_alternative = "greater",   root_tolerance = 1e-16 )

title: Compute \(v_S\) from Carlotti and Parast (2026) — compute_V_S_star
text: n Integer. Sample size. alpha Numeric. Type error rate (default 0.05). beta Numeric. Type II error rate (default 0.2). V_S_zero Numeric. hypothesized value surrogate's treatment effect null hypothesis (default 0.5). Numeric. First shape parameter alpha Beta prior (default 1). b Numeric. Second shape parameter beta Beta prior (default 1). BF_alternative Character. type alternative hypothesis: either "two_sided" "greater". root_tolerance Numeric. Tolerance level root-finding algorithm (default 1e-16).

title: Compute \(v_S\) from Carlotti and Parast (2026) — compute_V_S_star
text: list containing: BF_alpha: critical value Bayes factor corresponding specified alpha level. V_S_star: value \(v_S\) satisfies power constraint surrogate validation test.

title: Compute \(v_S\) from Carlotti and Parast (2026) — compute_V_S_star

text: MC_data list containing: P_observed: data frame matrix columns "Y" "S". Z: Treatment assignment vector. n1: Number treated units. n0: Number control units.

title: Monte Carlo Computation of the Parameter \(\delta\) from Parast et al. (2024) — compute_delta
text: list containing: U_Y: Mann-Whitney U statistic primary outcome Y computed P_observed. U_S: Mann-Whitney U statistic surrogate S computed P_observed. delta: difference U_Y - U_S.

title: Monte Carlo Computation of the Parameter \(\delta\) from Parast et al. (2024) — compute_delta
text: function processes data chosen data generating process, computing Mann-Whitney U statistic primary outcome \(Y\) surrogate \(S\): $$\hat{U}_Y = \frac{1}{n_1 n_0} \sum\limits_{:Z_i=1} \sum\limits_{j:Z_j=0} (Y_i > Y_j),$$ $$\hat{U}_S = \frac{1}{n_1 n_0} \sum\limits_{:Z_i=1} \sum\limits_{j:Z_j=0} (S_i > S_j).$$ , calculates $$\hat{\delta} = \hat{U}_Y - \hat{U}_S.$$ function generally intended called directly user instead used internally within BSET_no_X BSET_X.

title: Monte Carlo Computation of the Parameter \(\delta\) from Parast et al. (2024) — compute_delta
text: Parast L, Cai T, Tian L (2024). “rank-based approach evaluate surrogate marker small sample setting.” Biometrics, 80(1), ujad035.

title: Monte Carlo Computation of the Estimands for the Simulation Study in Carlotti and Parast (2026) — compute_estimands_Carlotti_and_Parast_2026
text: settings defined follows: Setting 1: X binary \(X = 1\):     $$Y_1 \sim \mathcal{N}(5, 1), \quad Y_0 \sim \mathcal{N}(0, 1)$$     $$S_1 = Y_1 + \mathcal{N}(0, 1), \quad S_0 = Y_0 + \mathcal{N}(0, 1)$$ \(X = 0\):     $$Y_1 \sim \mathcal{N}(5, 1), \quad Y_0 \sim \mathcal{N}(0, 1)$$     $$S_1 = Y_1 + \mathcal{N}(-10, 1), \quad S_0 = Y_0 + \mathcal{N}(-10, 1)$$ Setting 2: X Gaussian   $$(Y_1, S_1, Y_0, S_0) \mid X = x \sim \mathcal{N}(x (1, 7, 0, 6)', \frac{1}{2} I_4),$$ function generally intended called directly user. provided utility computing true parameter values simulation settings described Carlotti Parast (2026) .

title: Monte Carlo Computation of the Estimands for the Simulation Study in Parast et al. (2024) — compute_estimands_Parast_et_al_2024
text: data frame containing Monte Carlo estimates setting: setting: index simulation setting. U_Y_MC, U_S_MC, delta_MC: Parameters interest Parast et al. (2024) . V_Y_MC, V_S_MC, theta_MC: Parameters interest Carlotti Parast (2026) .

title: Monte Carlo Computation of the Estimands for the Simulation Study in Parast et al. (2024) — compute_estimands_Parast_et_al_2024
text: settings defined follows: Setting 1: Useless surrogate (Gaussian model)     $$Y_1 \sim \mathcal{N}(5, 3), \quad Y_0 \sim \mathcal{N}(3, 3),$$     $$S_1 \sim \mathcal{N}(2/3, 1), \quad S_0 \sim \mathcal{N}(2/3, 1).$$ Setting 2: Perfect surrogate (Gaussian model)     $$Y_1 \sim \mathcal{N}(6, 3), \quad Y_0 \sim \mathcal{N}(5/2, 3),$$     $$S_1 = Y_1 + \mathcal{N}(0, 1/10), \quad S_0 = Y_0 + \mathcal{N}(0, 1/10).$$ Setting 3: Imperfect surrogate (Gaussian model)     $$S_1 \sim \mathcal{N}(5, 3), \quad S_0 \sim \mathcal{N}(3, 3),$$     $$Y_1 = S_1 + \mathcal{N}(1.5, 0.6), \quad Y_0 = S_0 + \mathcal{N}(0, 0.6).$$ Setting 4: Misspecified model (non-Gaussian model)     $$S_1 \sim \exp(\mathcal{N}(2.5, 1.5)), \quad S_0 \sim \exp(\mathcal{N}(0.5, 1.5)),$$     $$Y_1 = 2 + 6/5 \sqrt{S_1} + 3/10 \exp(S_1 / 500) + \exp(\mathcal{N}(0, 0.3)),$$     $$Y_0 = 4/5 \sqrt{S_0} + 1/5 \exp(S_0 / 50) + \exp(\mathcal{N}(0, 0.3)).$$ function generally intended called directly user. provided utility computing true parameter values simulation settings described Parast et al. (2024) .

title: Monte Carlo Computation of the Parameter \(\theta\) from Carlotti and Parast (2026) — compute_theta
text: MC_data list containing: P: matrix data frame potential outcomes columns     "Y1", "Y0", "S1", "S0".

title: Monte Carlo Computation of the Parameter \(\theta\) from Carlotti and Parast (2026) — compute_theta
text: list containing true values: V_Y: Monte Carlo estimate \(P(Y_{1i} > Y_{0i})\) computed P. V_S: Monte Carlo estimate \(P(S_{1i} > S_{0i})\) computed P. theta: difference V_Y - V_S.

title: Monte Carlo Computation of the Parameter \(\theta\) from Carlotti and Parast (2026) — compute_theta
text: function processes data chosen data generating process, computing sample probabilities primary outcome \(Y\) surrogate \(S\): $$\hat{V}_Y = \frac{1}{n} \sum\limits^{n}_{=1} (Y_{1i} > Y_{0i}),$$ $$\hat{V}_S = \frac{1}{n} \sum\limits^{n}_{=1} (S_{1i} > S_{0i}).$$ , calculates $$\hat{\theta} = \hat{V}_Y - \hat{V}_S.$$ function generally intended called directly user instead used internally within BSET_no_X BSET_X.

title: Monte Carlo Computation of the Parameter \(\theta\) from Carlotti and Parast (2026) — compute_theta
text: Carlotti P, Parast L (2026). “Bayesian Critique Rank-Based Methods Surrogate Marker Evaluation.” arXiv preprint arXiv:2603.14381.

what: Frequentist Test from Parast et al. (2024) — frequentist_test
title: Frequentist Test from Parast et al. (2024) — frequentist_test
text: function performs frequentist test surrogate evaluation based rank-based methodology Parast et al. (2024) . calculates confidence intervals, \(\varepsilon\) threshold used, determines surrogate valid. function generally intended called directly user instead used internally within BSET_no_X BSET_X.

title: Frequentist Test from Parast et al. (2024) — frequentist_test
code: frequentist_test(P_observed, Z, alpha = 0.05, beta = 0.2, delta_true = NULL)

title: Frequentist Test from Parast et al. (2024) — frequentist_test
text: P_observed Observed outcomes \((Y, S)\) corresponding assigned treatment \(Z\). Z Treatment assignment vector. alpha Numeric. Significance level confidence interval (default 0.05). beta Numeric. Type II error rate (default 0.2). delta_true Numeric (optional). true value \(\delta\), used calculate frequentist coverage simulations.

title: Frequentist Test from Parast et al. (2024) — frequentist_test
text: list containing: CI: calculated confidence interval \(\delta\). epsilon: \(\varepsilon\) threshold value used test. coverage: Logical indicating delta_true falls within CI     (delta_true provided). power: Logical indicating upper bound CI epsilon, indicates test identifies surrogate valid.

title: Frequentist Test from Parast et al. (2024) — frequentist_test
text: Parast L, Cai T, Tian L (2024). “rank-based approach evaluate surrogate marker small sample setting.” Biometrics, 80(1), ujad035.
